items.title: Trump raises worldwide tariffs from 10% to 15% defying Supreme Court ruling | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>In the United States. President Donald Trump says he's raising a new tariff on imports from all of America's global trading partners from 10 to 15%.

Trump announced the new levy in response to a US Supreme Court ruling striking down most of his previous trade tariffs. Justices ruled they were imposed illegally. But the president insists the new 15% tariff has been legally tested.

00:00 Trump raises tariffs again
00:57 Carsten Brzeski, Chief Economist, ING Germany

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Thousands march at far-right protests in France after activist Quentin Deranque's death | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>In France, there are calls for calm as supporters gather in memory of a far-right activist who was killed last week. 

Quentin ‌Deranque died after being beaten during a street brawl between nationalist and left-wing groups in the southern city of Lyon. His death has become a rallying cry for the French right, and authorities fear rival extremists could clash at today's memorial march.

Controversy surrounding the activist's death has also spread beyond France's borders.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Israeli stikes kill at least 12 in Lebanon | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Amid rising regional tensions, two Israeli strikes in Lebanon have killed at least 12 people, marking one of the deadliest attacks since the November 2024 ceasefire that ended the war between Israel and Hezbollah — a truce both sides accuse each other of repeatedly violating. Among the dead were at least two members of Hamas and multiple Hezbollah fighters.

items.title: SCOTUS says no to Trump's tariffs: What happens next? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Donald Trump is imposing a new 10 percent global tariff on America's trading partners after the Supreme Court ruled most of his previous levies were illegal. Trump sharply criticized the 6-3 ruling and accused the Supreme Court of being "swayed by foreign interests."

00:00 Trump unhappy about SCOTUS tariffs ruling
02:29 DW speaks with Rebecca Christie, a senior fellow at the Brussels-based Bruegel economic policy think tank

items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Up to 10,000 Danes protested US President Trump after the US president accused NATO ally Denmark of avoiding frontline combat in the joint Afghanistan mission. Forty-four Danish soldiers died there.

items.title: Canals power global trade at a growing cost | Transforming Business
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>They are drivers of global trade, but they also displace rural activities, require large volumes of water, and transform ecosystems. From Turkey to France to Panama, many canals and waterways face the same dilemma.
Chapters: 
0:00 – Intro:  
00:30 Chapter 1 – Why canals matter?  
02:18 Chapter 2 – Promises of progress - at what cost?  
05:45 Chapter 3 – Offsetting the environmental impact  
07:45 Chapter 4 – Living with climate change  
10:25 Chapter 5 – Can canals work with nature?
12:05 Outro

items.title: Trump's new tariffs: What they mean for consumers, markets and Europe | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Donald Trump wants to impose a 10 percent global tariff on America's trading partners after the Supreme Court ruled most of his previous levies have been illegal. Trump said the 6-3 ruling left him “deeply disappointed” and accused the Supreme Court of being "swayed by foreign interests”. The US president vowed to deploy new methods to impose tariffs, including a sweeping new duty on all foreign trading partners. DW's Steven Beardsley reports from the studio and Rosie Birchard reports from Brussels.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: European fighter jet might be canceled due to France and Germany's disagreement | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>France and Germany are at odds over a new generation of fighter aircraft still in development. The French armed forces insist the jet must be able to carry nuclear arms, but for the Germans that's irrelevant because they don't have the weapons. A German-dominated aerospace firm has expressed doubts about the future of the multi-billion euro project.

Chapters:
0:00 France and Germany disagree on FCAS
1:52 Thomas Wiegold, Defense analyst

items.title: How White House's demands for Ukraine put Ukraine under the cosh | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Nearly four years after Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine, Volodymyr Zelenskyy, the President of the war-torn country, insists his side is not losing the war. Speaking to foreign media ahead of the grim anniversary, Zelenskyy said US-brokered talks in Geneva had made progress on military issues, but left the question of territory unresolved. He added that if the talks lead to a ceasefire, the United States would play a key role in monitoring any agreement.

Chapters:
0:00 Zelenskyy says Ukraine has not lost way
0:47 Alexander Lord, Europe-Eurasia Analyst

items.content:
